dossiers.business: Global insurance broker + health/wealth/career benefits consulting.
dossiers.thesis: Quality broker in a 4-player oligopoly at a peer discount after a guide-down — but the laggard of the group. CONTESTED: insider/Pershing flags UNCONFIRMED on current data.
dossiers.decline_explanation: Down ~27% from high. Q1 2026 (Apr 30): beat EPS but cut Risk&Broking organic growth to mid-single-digits on missed new-business + softening commercial pricing cycle; consulting cut to low/mid-single with no near-term recovery. Structurally the weakest of the big-4 brokers (trails Marsh/Aon). NOTE: screen's insider-buying + Pershing-Square flags could NOT be verified — recent Form 4s are routine RSU grants, and WTW is absent from Pershing's Q1-2026 13F.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Largest US natural-gas producer (Chesapeake+Southwestern merger); Appalachia/Haynesville.
dossiers.thesis: Premier large-cap gas pure-play levered to LNG/data-center demand, cheap on normalized gas. CONTESTED: commodity price-taker into a likely 2026+ gas/LNG glut with no permanent CEO.
dossiers.decline_explanation: Down ~30% from high. Henry Hub fell to ~$3.29 on strong production + warm-weather demand worries; EIA cut 2026/27 gas forecasts (~4.4%/11.5%) amid an LNG-glut narrative; Barclays downgraded to Equalweight. Governance overhang: CEO Dell'Osso stepped down Feb 2026, interim CEO + ongoing search + HQ relocation. Insider buying is real (interim CEO + director near lows) but it's a bet on commodity normalization, not a company fix.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Small-cap non-operated oil & gas E&P; minority working interests across US basins.
dossiers.thesis: 2027 free-cash-flow inflection story with ~8% yield and strong insider buying (37 buys/0 sells). CONTESTED→knife-risk: non-op micro-cap price-taker with a dividend not currently covered by FCF.
dossiers.decline_explanation: Down ~25% from high / near range low. Soft oil & gas prices pressuring small-cap E&P; deliberate growth slowdown (2026 guide ~9-12% vs 28% in 2025). Dividend is the fault line: paid while loss-making with no current FCF, implying balance-sheet funding; ~8% yield screens as distress. Non-op model = passive minority partner with no control over operator pace/capex. FCF promise is a 2027 'trust us' story. Insider signal is the cleanest of the energy names (sponsor-linked director, 37 buys/0 sells).
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Owns UFC, WWE, IMG — live-sports media rights and events.
dossiers.thesis: Quasi-monopoly live-sports content with rights-renewal tailwinds; in-the-money C-suite cluster buy. CONTESTED: near all-time highs at ~79x P/E after two straight misses — paying full freight.
dossiers.decline_explanation: Not a decline — trades ~5% off its high. Flagged on insiders, not distress. Minor Feb-2026 wobble (FY26 revenue guide below ~$6.0B consensus; Q1 EPS $1.12 missed $1.19) but recovered. Bear case is purely valuation (~79x trailing P/E, 4.8x book) with zero room for error, lumpy rights-renewal growth that decelerates once big UFC/WWE deals lap, and Emanuel/Silver Lake key-man + overhang. May-13 cluster buy real and in-the-money (CEO Emanuel ~$1.29M @ $185; Shapiro, CFO, Kraft) — but trailing-12m insider flow is net selling.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Puerto Rico payments/transaction processor with a growing Latin America segment.
dossiers.thesis: Cheap (~13x), profitable, double-digit-growth processor with real insider buying into the decline — the cleanest 'insider-into-weakness' of the set. CONTESTED: PR + FX-dependent LatAm concentration justify part of the discount.
dossiers.decline_explanation: Down ~28% from high (hit ~36% at the June low). Slow bleed, not a crash: Business Solutions EBITDA margin -370bps to 35.3% in Q4-2025; market doubts durability of 36% LatAm growth (4pts was FX) and fears a cyclical/FX peak; modest Q1-2026 miss. But management RAISED FY26 guidance after Q1, so this is multiple compression, not a guidance cut. Real insider buying near current levels (EVP Vizcarrondo 21k @ ~$23.37 — first buy after 4 sales; Director Smith ~$428k @ ~$26.4), though trailing-12m flow still net selling.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Resolute Holdings Management — a 7-employee external asset manager spun from CompoSecure (NOT Ramaco Resources).
dossiers.thesis: Capital-light fee stream run by activist operators with roll-up ambition. FALLING KNIFE: misidentified ticker, richly valued, illiquid micro-float, related-party conflicts, bear target ~$26.
dossiers.decline_explanation: Down ~43% from a Jan-2026 high after a 335% post-spin bubble popped (multiple de-rate, not an operating blowup — Q1 fees inflected positive). DATA-INTEGRITY FLAG: ticker resolves to Resolute Holdings Management (ex-CompoSecure spin, Feb 2025), NOT Ramaco Resources. ~$1.1B cap, only ~8.3M shares / ~4M float, quoted bid/ask $100-$213 — effectively un-investable and 'price' is unreliable. Value depends almost entirely on managing CompoSecure (circular/related-party risk); credible bear re-rate targets ~$26. Insider buys are token (~$70k each) and underwater.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Insulet — Omnipod tubeless wearable insulin patch-pump (~99% of revenue).
dossiers.thesis: 30%+ grower at a 57% discount with broad insider buying. CONTESTED→knife: two life-critical recalls in ten weeks + credible H2-2026 tubeless competition are real thesis-killers on a single-product company.
dossiers.decline_explanation: Down ~57% from high despite a strong Q1 (rev +34%, EPS beat, guidance RAISED). Two voluntary Omnipod-5 under-delivery corrections in ten weeks — Mar 12 and a far larger May 26 recall of ~7M pods (~8.5% of 2025 output) with 24 serious adverse events incl. DKA — hint at a systemic quality problem (FDA/litigation/brand risk). May 28: appeals court overturned a $452M trade-secret win vs EOFlow. Tubeless competition arriving H2-2026 (Tandem Mobi, Medtronic MiniMed Flex) threatens ~72% gross margins; GLP-1s cap long-run pump demand. Insiders buying near lows (directors flat, new CEO ~38% underwater).
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Fast-casual Mediterranean chain ('next Chipotle'); company-owned unit growth.
dossiers.thesis: Best-in-class fast-casual momentum (Q1 SSS +9.7% on +6.8% traffic). NOT A BARGAIN: barely fell (-15%), ~150x P/E priced for perfection; insider 'buys' are token vs a wall of founder/CEO selling.
dossiers.decline_explanation: Not a real decline — only ~15% off the high and still +58% YTD; flagged on insider buying, not distress. Bear case is valuation: ~140-175x trailing P/E, ~63-67x EV/EBITDA (industry median ~9.5x), priced for flawless execution on volatile comps (Q4-2025 was +0.5% before Q1's +9.7%). The two insider buys (COO $318k @ ~$79.45; CLO $70k @ ~$70) are tiny and underwater, set against heavy persistent founder/CEO selling (~50 sells / 0 buys; Chairman Shaich ~2.4M sh). Insider signal here is effectively noise.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Universal Display — licenses phosphorescent-OLED emitter IP + sells emitter materials.
dossiers.thesis: Monopoly emitter toll-booth at a 46% discount with a ~$1.5M insider cluster + blue-PHOLED catalyst near. CONTESTED→knife: patent cliff (2027-28), 42% customer concentration, perpetually-delayed catalyst, buyers underwater.
dossiers.decline_explanation: Down ~46%, just above the 52-wk low. Q1-2026: revenue $142.2M (from $166.3M); high-margin royalties collapsed to $54.2M from $73.6M (-26%) on adverse customer mix; EPS $0.76 vs $1.35; FY26 guidance CUT to $630-670M. Structural overhangs: core emitter patents start expiring ~2027-2028 (erodes the royalty toll-booth); extreme concentration (one customer ~42% of 9M-2025 revenue); blue-PHOLED catalyst promised 2024→2H25, still no TV shipped (2027+ realistic); TADF/tandem competition + BOE litigation. Insider cluster ~$1.5M (CEO/CLO/director) but all underwater @ ~$92-95, from the same team whose guidance keeps disappointing.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Celsius Holdings — energy-drink platform (Celsius + Alani Nu + Rockstar) via PepsiCo distribution.
dossiers.thesis: Cheap 3-brand platform with incumbent-management buying the dip. CONTESTED: the original explosive-organic-growth thesis is broken; now a lower-margin roll-up in a crowded, private-label-threatened category.
dossiers.decline_explanation: Down ~55% from high. Slow-motion de-rate: Q1-2026 record revenue $782.6M but flagship Celsius grew only ~6% and gross margin fell to 48.3% from 52.3% on dilutive acquired-brand mix + aluminum costs (stock -10.7% on May 11). Late-March: Costco launched a Kirkland private-label energy drink (margin/competition fear). The core single-premium-brand growth thesis has quietly become a structurally lower-margin roll-up (Alani Nu, Rockstar) in a crowded category vs Monster/Red Bull. Real but modest incumbent buying (CEO Fieldly ~$249k, President Hanson, ~$700k total, no sales), roughly flat vs current.
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: Copart — online salvage/total-loss vehicle auctions, primarily for auto insurers.
dossiers.thesis: Best-in-class auction duopolist at the cheapest multiple in years, ASPs +5% showing pricing power. CONTESTED: whether falling insurance total-loss VOLUME is cyclical or a structural underinsurance shift is the open question; 13F signal is mixed.
dossiers.decline_explanation: Down ~39% from the 52-wk high (~52% off all-time high). Volume, not pricing: Q2-FY26 EPS $0.36 missed $0.39, revenue ~$1.12B -3.6% YoY on declining total-loss insurance volumes — fewer claims, hurricane base effect, and consumers dropping collision coverage as premiums rise (underinsurance). Q3 global insurance units -2.7%. ASPs +5% partly offset. The bear question is cyclical vs structural impairment of the volume engine; a high-multiple compounder being repriced as a low-growth cyclical. 13F mixed: concentrated conviction (Gobi Capital ~7.7% of book) vs net hedge-fund exits (Renaissance selling).
dossiers.author: oracle

dossiers.business: EquipmentShare.com — construction-equipment rental + jobsite/fleet software (T3); IPO'd Jan 2026.
dossiers.thesis: Fast-growing (rental +34%) rental+software story founder-led with big insider ownership. FALLING KNIFE: unprofitable post-IPO, ~$3B debt, cash burn with dilution risk; screen's 'activist 13D' is actually the founder.
dossiers.decline_explanation: Down ~41% from its Jan-29 post-IPO peak (IPO'd Jan 13 @ $24.50). Classic falling-knife profile: unprofitable, ~$3.1-3.3B long-term debt + ~$999M ABL drawn, ~$70M/qtr interest, Q1-2026 net losses, analysts warning of <1yr cash absent improved cash flow (dilution/balance-sheet risk). Competes vs entrenched profitable giants (United Rentals, Ashtead) in a cyclical, rate-exposed industry. FLAG CORRECTION: the screen's 'activist 13D' is NOT an outside activist — it's co-founder/CEO Jabbok Schlacks disclosing a 16.2% stake; founders control ~81% of votes (dual-class), so minority holders have little recourse. Director Hill bought ~$499k @ $22.89 (real) but it doesn't de-risk the balance sheet.
dossiers.author: oracle
